entry.title: Remove SOAP envelope and namespace prefix using XSLT mapping in SAP CPI
entry.content:
Hello Sapians,

Sometimes, we encountered an issue where CPI is getting data from SOAP sender channel which have SOAP envelope and namespace prefix in it and we face issue while processing that. Especially if we need to route the flow as per the condition. In that case, we need to read the value coming in SOAP fields and process further. The XML condition of router will not get processed until the SOAP envelope and namespace prefix are removed.

To resolve this issue, we can use XSLT mapping which can remove the entire SOAP envelope and namespace prefix to process further in SAP CPI.

Sample SOAP payload :

<?xml version="1.0"?>
<soap:Envelope xmlns:soap="http://www.w3.org/2003/05/soap-envelope" soap:encodingStyle="http://www.w3.org/2003/05/soap-encoding">
<soap:Body>
<m:GetPrice xmlns:m="https://www.w3schools.com/prices">
<m:Item>Apples</m:Item>
<m:Price>110</m:Price>
</m:GetPrice>
</soap:Body>
</soap:Envelope>

XSLT mapping to remove namespace and prefix.

<xsl:stylesheet version="1.0" xmlns:xsl="http://www.w3.org/1999/XSL/Transform">
<xsl:output method="xml" omit-xml-declaration="yes" />
<xsl:template match="/|comment()|processing-instruction()">
<xsl:copy>
<xsl:apply-templates />
</xsl:copy>
</xsl:template>
<xsl:template match="*">
<xsl:element name="{local-name()}">
<xsl:apply-templates select="@*|node()" />
</xsl:element>
</xsl:template>
<xsl:template match="@*">
<xsl:attribute name="{local-name()}">
<xsl:value-of select="." />
</xsl:attribute>
</xsl:template>
</xsl:stylesheet>

 

Output payload :

 

<Envelope encodingStyle="http://www.w3.org/2003/05/soap-encoding">
    <Body>
        <GetPrice>
            <Item>Apples</Item>
            <Price>110</Price>
        </GetPrice>
    </Body>
</Envelope>
 

This conclude the blog😊, with this the SOAP envelope and namespace prefix will be removed in SAP CPI.

In case of any queries, please comment down. Will try to resolve your concerns😊.

entry.title: Re: INT4 doesn't accept negative value
entry.content:
I had the same problem with INT4. I did a cast to quan. That did the trick for me:

cast (DATS_DAYS_BETWEEN( coalesce( Versand.CreationDate, SDIA.CreationDate ), SDIA.CreationDate) as abap.quan(10, 0 ) )

entry.title: SAP Business Transformation Center: End to End Digital Blueprint creation
entry.content:
SAP Business Transformation Center: End to End Digital Blueprint creation

What is Business Transformation Center (BTC)?

Companies who wants to go with Bluefield conversion (Selective Data Transition) approach, SAP has given a new Tool to selectively migrate their Master and Transactional data for respective company codes only not for all. The new tool is Business Transformation Center (BTC).

 

BTC Tool and its process is only supported for Bluefields Projects (Selective Data Transition) not for Brownfield and Greenfield Projects. It can work for On premise and On Cloud (Public) solutions only.

 

In this blog I’ll show how to create Digital Blueprint in SAP BTC Tool “Company Code wise”.

How Business Transformation Center (BTC) tool works?

For  End to End and Technical configuration of BTC Tool, please refer the Blog publishes by my colleague.

SDT Using SAP Business Transformation Center: End ... - SAP Community

 

Company Code wise Data Migration.

BTC tool has 3 major phases:

  • Selective Data Transition: Define the Scope of your Transition
  • Selective Data Transition: Model your Transformation and
  • Selective Data Transition: Execute and Monitor your Transformation

 

In this blog we will only cover “Selective Data Transition: Define the Scope of your Transition”  Digital Blueprint creation part.

  • Selective Data Transition: Define the Scope of your Transition

The first task in BTC Tool is to do the Scoping which can be done through “Selective Data Transition: Define the Scope of your Transformation app”.

 

The first two apps “Manage Analysis Files and Manage System Scan” are Basis activity.

Once the Analysis File is uploaded and system scanning is done next step is to create a Digital Blueprint. Digital Blueprint is a heart of BTC tool process, without Digital Blueprint scoping and the Selective Data Migration is not possible in S4 HANA system.

Before creation of Digital Blueprint, we need to have a detailed workshop with business on organization AS IS business process. After the ASI IS workshop with business is completed, we can then discuss with business on the Digital Blueprint scoping like what company codes  which company codes will be In scope and Out of Scope. Only the In-scope company codes data will be moved to S4 HANA system. The out-of-scope company codes will not be moved to S4 HANA system.

 

Digital Blueprint creation Process

We can create Digital Blueprint using “Manage Digital Blueprint App”

 

Click on Create and fill the necessary details.

 

 

 

 

The system will show us the list of all the company codes from source system.

 

 

 

 

Navigate to Company Code App and click on Mass Edit and choose the company codes to be in scope and out of scope

 

 

In my test case I took 4 company codes in Scope out of 80 and rest 76 are out of scope

AR01, HK01,TH01 and PH01

 

 

We can also see the In scope company code in ‘Mange Digital Blueprint” also. We can also choose Transformation Objects based on business requirement and can make the objects In scope or out of scope. 

We can navigate to Select Transformation app by selecting the Related Apps from Select Company Code app itself and click on Select Transformation Objects

 

 

 

System will show you all the Transformation objects in scope initially. In our case Total Transformation Objects were 169

We can discuss with business what Transformation objects to be kept in Scope or out of scope by referring last activity and Total Data Count.

We can exclude data whose Total Data Count= “0” or we can also check if last activity for that objects are very old.

As seen we have chosen all the 169 Transformation Objects in Scope for our 4 company codes which can be seen in the app

 

 

The Select Scanned table in “The Select Scanned Table in the  Selective Data Transition: Define the Scope of your Transition” app will list all the standard and custom tables in the Digital Blue Print

 

We can further navigate to Digital Blueprint overview app to get the graphical detail of the Company Code Selection, Transformation object Selection and so on.

 

 

 

The final step is to confirm the Digital blueprint when all the above scoping is done with business. This step is mandatory to move to the next step of Preparation and Execution of Data Migration

Note: Once Digital Blueprint is confirmed it cannot be changed further. For any new requirement new Digital Blueprint has to be created every time.

Hope this blog has helped you in understanding of how Digital Blueprint works in SAP BTC tool as a starting step for data migration to S4 HANA system.

entry.title: Re: How to create a RAP BO to change the content of a customizing table in the production client
entry.content:
@ShyamPrasad 

The generated objects (ABAP class, behavior definition, CDS view, ...) are always recorded on a workbench request and can therefore be transported to other clients.
"No Transport" only means that the generated application has no select transport action and does not record the changes regarding the content of the configuration table in a customizing request.
In addition, the adaptable in production setting is set to true so that you can change the configuration table in the test and production tenants in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.
To change the adaptable in production setting, open the transport object definition in ADT:

entry.title: Standalone As-Built Configuration in SAP ME - Excess component Qty removal
entry.content:
Hi All,

I have stuck at a point while developing a custom solution for Excess qty removal for components in SFCs.

Issue: Due to changes in BOM in S4 after order release and start of execution in SAP ME, there is an error during the completion of the operation "Check config for <<SFC>> reports <<QTY>> missing <<component>>, 0 excess, 0 non-BOM and 0 test part Components (Message 13920)". upon checking, found that due to changes, there is an excess qty for the component is available, using standalone As-Built configuration activity, we were able to return the excess qty for an SFC but it's taking huge efforts to return for each SFC.

So, I have started developing a solution where the input will be the shop order and all the SFCs with the excess qty in the component should be returned.
I'm able to fetch the excess qty info from SFC_ASSY & SFC_ASSY_LOG tables.

I tried to use removeComponents PAPI but getting an error while executing the MII transaction.

can you please let me know if there is any standard service/solution is available to return the excess qty.

Thank You!

entry.title: Re: When will Joule be released also in Italian language?
entry.content:
Hello, 

Currently there is no publically available date showing availability for Italian. This can change over time so I recommend checking the roadmap viewer closer to the next release. I include the link below. 

The languages that will currently be supported in the H1 2025 release are visible in this document. As you can see in the document there is a note stating that even if a language is not supported the AI can often still understand the question and will give a response in English.  See the guide below. 
https://help.sap.com/docs/joule/joule-capabilities/8d44120a69844638ad160569b8bdd60e.html?state=DRAFT&version=DEV

From the guide "Because Joule relies on a Large Language Model, Joule can understand languages other than those officially supported. If you send a request in a language other than those supported by Joule, it is possible that Joule will understand the request and provide a response. However, if Joule is able to understand the request, the response will be provided in the language set by the associated SAP application."

Roadmap Viewer showing Upcoming features and timelines -
https://roadmaps.sap.com/board?range=CURRENT-LAST&PRODUCT=40F2E9281A631EDA94C7C01F9A1F40F4#Q2%202025 

If you feel there is an urgent need for this language to be supported you can submit an influence request.  https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/3768 

Paul

entry.title: Request for Reconsideration of Yearly Certification Renewal Policy – SAP ABAP on HANA (C_TAW12_750)
entry.content:
Greetings!

I am writing to express a concern shared by many senior SAP consultants, including myself, who specialize in ABAP on HANA and are planning for certification.

About Me:

  • SAP ABAP Consultant with over 9 years of hands-on project experience.
  • Proficient in Core ABAP, ABAP on HANA, ODATA, Adobe Forms, and SAP Script.
  • Currently preparing for the SAP Certified Development Associate - ABAP for SAP HANA 2.0 (Exam Code: C_TAW12_750).

Learning Path Completed:

  • TAW10e — ABAP Fundamentals
  • TAW12e — Advanced ABAP on HANA
  • HA100 — SAP HANA Introduction
  • HA400 — ABAP Programming for SAP HANA

My career goal is to continue working in ABAP & HANA for the next decade without shifting to other technologies like Fiori, RAP, or Cloud-only models.

Key Concern: Given my commitment to working in the same technology for the foreseeable future, I question the necessity of paying substantial fees annually for certification renewal or mandatory Learning Hub subscriptions.

Challenges Faced by Consultants:

  • Lack of reimbursement from most employers.
  • No salary increase or bonus tied to certification.
  • Absence of financial support from employers.

Arguments Against Yearly Renewal:

  1. ABAP is a mature and stable language.
  2. Core ABAP & ABAP on HANA do not undergo yearly changes like Cloud products.
  3. Why should we pay annually if we are working on the same release and technology?
  4. Renewal should be based on need, not time.
  5. The current renewal policy appears to be more of a revenue-generation model than a knowledge validation process.
  6. Consultants working in support or maintenance projects may not use new features.
  7. SAP does not offer any special benefits for paying yearly renewal fees.

Suggestions for SAP:

  • Introduce Lifetime Certification for stable SAP modules like ABAP.
  • Require renewal only when a major version change occurs (e.g., S/4HANA Cloud RAP mandatory knowledge).
  • Reduce Learning Hub costs for individual consultants.
  • Offer special Learning Hub pricing for self-learners or freelancers.
  • Recognize long-term consultants with experience-based recognition badges.

Final Question: Why should consultants pay yearly fees to validate their knowledge in a technology where no new learning or project exposure is required?

I just want to comment on the conclusion. as per the logic described in the examples shouldn't we  highlight the conclusion this way : 

  • If the Skip Simplified Sourcing is unchecked, Contracts and Scheduling Agreements will be automatically selected as a source of supply.
  • If the Skip Simplified Sourcing is unchecked, Contracts and Scheduling Agreements will only be selected as a source of supply if there is a source list entry determining that they should be selected by MRP.

Kr,

entry.title: Re: Hello, I have a dump in transaction MFBF
entry.content:
Hello @David_mT 

It looks like you are using a batch determination quantity proposal procedure 600 which is not defined in your system. That is strange as procedure 600 is part of standard delivery:

************************************************************************
* FORM CHMVS_600
************************************************************************
* Routine to distribute the available quantities of batches.
* The distribution is an example for a TOP-DOWN distribution
* with regard to the sort sequence.
* To set up your own distribution rules, sort the following table
* according to your requirements.
*
* Structure of internal table DISQTY (significant fields):
* --------------------------------------------------------
*    DATA: BEGIN OF DISQTY OCCURS 0,
*            MATNR     LIKE BDBATCH-MATNR,    -> material number
*            CHARG     LIKE BDBATCH-CHARG,    -> batch number
*            WERKS     LIKE BDBATCH-WERKS,    -> plant
*            LGORT     LIKE BDBATCH-LGORT,    -> storage location
*            AVAL_QUAN LIKE V01FDK-VRFMG,     -> available quantity
*            QUANTITY  LIKE BDBATCH-MENGE,    -> target quantity
*            RES_TABIX LIKE SY-TABIX.         ->internal use
*    DATA: END OF DISQTY.
*
* Additional fields for distribution:
* -----------------------------------
* NO_OF_SPLIT -> The number of possible batch quantity splits
* QUAN_TO_DIS -> The quantity to be distributed (ordered quantity)
*
* Restrictions!
* -------------
* 1) Don't add or delete any entries from the internal table
* 2) Don't modify fields MATNR, CHARG, WERKS, LGORT, AVAL_QUAN and
*    RES_TABIX.
* 3) Don't change values of NO_OF_SPLIT and QUAN_TO_DIS
* 4) The only field that can be modified is the DISQTY-QUANTITY field
************************************************************************
FORM chmvs_600.

* BEGIN: get valuation type from PO line item
* the logic copied from the email. Can't verify it without access to Yara system.
* Assuming it is correct

  DATA: lv_delnr  TYPE lips-vbeln,
        lv_delpos TYPE lips-posnr,
        lv_ebeln  TYPE ekpo-ebeln,
        lv_ebelp  TYPE ekpo-ebelp.

...

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: VM status is Active for SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02 & SAP...
entry.content:
Hi WritwikIERPA ,

 

According to our log files the main reason for the VM status is that you have requested more vCPU capacity than your current vCPU limit of 44 allows for the instance bucket that the specified instance type belongs to.

You could adjust your quota and then to try again. It could take some time for the changes to take effect. 

Please also note that the number of used by you vCPUs has been calculated as an sum off all vCPUs of your SAP Cloud Appliance Library appliances and also of these, started directly via the Cloud provider Console.

For more information, see our FAQ -> How to increase your AWS Service quotas? question.

 

Best regards,

Tsvetinka

entry.title: Re: Are the SF LMS course completion timestamp a trusted\...
entry.content:
Hi, 

I think you are asking two questions here. 

1- Can the completion status in the learning system be trusted as accurate - the answer here would be yes?

2- Can these completion status be used as to meet the requirements of of a specific EU Funding Program?
This is a question that can only be answered by you comparing the exact requirements of Funding program and what is acceptable to be submitted vs the information that the system can provide. 
So its something that should be checked with your company's legal department. 

You could also consider the E-Signature functionality which is what is used for some industries that require strict compliance requirements. However, as stated above whether it meets the requirements of a specific program or not needs to be checked against those stated requirements by your company. E-signature information. - https://help.sap.com/docs/successfactors-learning/creating-learning-approval-processes/how-sap-successfactors-learning-uses-e-signatures 
Paul

entry.title: Re: How to dynamically set the basic authentication for t...
entry.content:
Hi Frank!

 

Assuming that the KVM looks like this:

nameOfMyKVM

dynamicUsername*****
dynamicPassword*****

 

You can add a Key Value Map Operations Policy to your API Proxy to access the values:

<KeyValueMapOperations mapIdentifier="nameOfMyKVM" async="true" continueOnError="false" enabled="true" xmlns="http://www.sap.com/apimgmt">
    <Get assignTo="private.usernameFromKVM" index="1">
        <Key>
            <Parameter>dyanmicUsername</Parameter>
        </Key>
    </Get>
    <Get assignTo="private.passwordFromKVM" index="1">
        <Key>
            <Parameter>dynamicPassword</Parameter>
        </Key>
    </Get>
    <Scope>environment</Scope>
</KeyValueMapOperations>

(Note that you won't see variables starting with the prefix "private." in the debug mode of the API Proxy.)

 

Now you can add a Basic Authentication Policy to assign the variables "private.usernameFromKVM" and "private.passwordFromKVM" to the Authorization header of your request.

<BasicAuthentication async='true' continueOnError='false' enabled='true' xmlns='http://www.sap.com/apimgmt'>
	<Operation>Encode</Operation>
	<IgnoreUnresolvedVariables>false</IgnoreUnresolvedVariables>
	<User ref='private.usernameFromKVM'></User>
	<Password ref='private.passwordFromKVM'></Password>
 	<AssignTo>request.header.Authorization</AssignTo>
</BasicAuthentication>

entry.title: Re: Can somene help me how to deactivate SAP GTS to the P...
entry.content:
Hi,

please check customizing function "Control Data for Transfer to SAP Global Trade Services" (ECC) or "Configure Control Settings for Document Transfer" (S/4HANA).
You can deactivate transfer of Purchase Orders for Compliance Checks on Application Level MM0A for single document types or document type/company code.

Additionally, if you use SAP S/4HANA, you can deactivate function "prevent GR for blocked purchase documents" in this customizing path.

BR
Fabian

entry.title: Now Available: Manage Your SAP Business AI Features Flexibly from a Single Place
entry.content:
We’re excited to announce that in close collaboration with SAP Business AI, SAP for Me and SAP Central Business Configuration, AI-assisted scoping and configuration services we now offer a tailored view on AI features that matter for you.

You can access this view in SAP for Me under Portfolio & Products and then Business AI. It includes the AI features that are relevant to you, depending on the SAP solutions you’re running. For now, we’re guiding you along the creation of a support case to activate AI features easily and fast. While this is a beta version, you will soon be able to instantly activate or deactivate and test out AI features with the touch of a finger and unleash direct value in seconds.

 

Screenshot of SAP for Me, Product & Portfolio, Business AI

Screenshot of Support Case Creation

This free-of-charge service is a game-changer for you as SAP cloud customers, providing you with the autonomy and flexibility to manage your SAP Business AI features according to your specific business needs centrally.

Stay tuned for more!

entry.title: Bring Your Own Identity: SAP's Strategic Transformation of Identity and Authentication Management
entry.content:
We are excited to announce the foundation for Bring Your Own Identity (BYOI) by Identity Renovation, an innovative transformation designed to enhance interactions with SAP's digital platforms. 

Our vision is to enable straightforward digital interactions and processes for customers from their initial contact to known user status, consistent across all digital touchpoints and cloud products. This approach will be implemented without the constraints of scaling, performance, compliance, and security issues inherent in legacy identity management systems.

Customers and partners adopting Bring Your Own Identity will gain complete control over user management, ensuring smooth integration and increased security. They can tailor Identity & Access Management (IAM) setups to their specific needs while ensuring compliance and mitigating risks of data breaches in a cloud-ready environment.

This initiative represents a strategic enhancement designed to improve interactions with SAP's digital platforms by allowing customers and partners to connect their Corporate Identity Providers (IdPs) with SAP. Our development teams are actively working on its implementation to make authentication more efficient across all SAP touchpoints, so stay tuned for updates.

To facilitate this transition, we are optimizing our Identity & Access Management (IAM) systems for seamless registration and integration of Corporate Identity Providers. This will enable customers and partners to use their existing corporate credentials, providing unified access to both corporate applications and the SAP ecosystem.

We are undertaking these comprehensive changes to meet the growing expectations of our customers and partners for a modern, secure, and cohesive digital experience with SAP. This effort is crucial not only to ensure up-to-date security but also to provide smooth user interactions and build trust across our digital platforms. The goal is for customer identities to be onboarded once and utilized across all SAP products and digital services, enabling secure and compliant management of authorizations.

Implementing Bring Your Own Identity is a detailed process. To ensure a smooth transition, we are planning a pilot phase with a selected group of pilot customers starting in Q3 2025. Following the pilot, we aim for a global rollout later in 2026. This phased approach will help us refine and perfect the system to better meet the needs of all our users worldwide.

The target audience for the global rollout includes all customers and partners equipped with their own corporate Identity Provider and prepared to embrace Bring Your Own Identity.

Find more information about the topic on YouTube: How Identity Renovation will Simplify User Authentication at SAP Touchpoints in the Future

Join our Continuous Influence Session to share detailed ideas and requirements on Bring Your Own Identity, S-user Management, and SAP Universal ID, ensuring clarity and effective review by SAP. Please note that an S-user is required to submit improvement requests.

entry.title: SAP Intercompany Processes: STO with Billing step by step guideline
entry.content:
The Intercompany Stock Transfer Order (STO) process in SAP enables organizations to manage the transfer of goods between different company codes while ensuring proper valuation, taxation, and financial reconciliation.

When billing is integrated into the intercompany STO process, it ensures that the supplying entity invoices the receiving entity, maintaining compliance with transfer pricing regulations and providing a clear audit trail for cross-company transactions.

In this blog, we will explore the key configuration steps, master data requirements, and process flow for setting up and executing an Intercompany STO with Billing in SAP.

Steps to be carried out:

1. Create Intercompany Stock Transfer order (ME21N)

  • Vendor (Receiving company, set up an intercompany vendor)
  • Plant (Shipping & Receiving)
  • Save to Generate the Intercompany STO Number.

2. Outbound Delivery Creation (VL10D)

  • Reference the STO to Create the Outbound Delivery
  • Enter the Pick Qty & from Storage Location
  • Post Goods Issue (GI) (MIGO) to reduce stock in the supplying plant

3. Goods Receipt (GR) at Receiving Plant (MIGO)

  • The receiving company posts Goods Receipt (GR) to update its inventory

4. Post Invoice Document (MIRO)

  • The receiving company posts the Invoice Verification

5. Post Billing Document (VF01)

  • Create billing document (invoice) from the outbound delivery:
  • Pricing is determined based on intercompany conditions.

Step 1: Create Supplier in the Receiving Company Code

Step 2: Create Customer in the Supplying Company Code

Maintain Shipping Condition under Sales and Distribution view under shipping tab.

Step 3: Create Material and Extend for both the plants

Step 4: Define Shipping Point

Path: SPRO -> Enterprise Structure –> Definition – >Logistics Execution –> Define, Copy, Delete, Check shipping point

Step 5: Assign Shipping Point to Plant

Path: SPRO –> Enterprise Structure –> Assignment –> Logistics Execution –> Assign shipping point to plant.

Step 6: Shipping Point Determination

Path: SPRO–> Logistics Execution –> Shipping –> Basic Shipping Functions –> Shipping Point and Goods Receiving Point Determination –> Assign Shipping Points

Step 7: Define Shipping Data for Plants

Path: SPRO –> Material Management –> Purchasing –> Purchase order –> Set up Stock Transport order –> Define Shipping Data for Plants

For Supplying Plant

For Receiving Plant

Step 8: Assign Delivery Type & Checking Rule

Path: SPRO –> Material Management –> Purchasing –> Purchase order –> Set up Stock Transport order –> Assign Delivery Type and Checking Rule

Step 9: Assign Document Type, One Step Procedure, Under Delivery Tolerance

Path: SPRO –> Material Management –> Purchasing –> Purchase order –> Set up Stock Transport order –> Assign Document Type, One-Step Procedure, Under delivery Tolerance.

Note: If one step check box ticked, In this process as soon as the supplying plant posted Goods Issue with 645 Mvt. type, 101 (GR) in receiving plant will be posted immediately with same material document number.

Step 10: Create Stock Transfer Order

STO 9900051 Created

Step 11: Create Outbound Delivery with STO Reference

Outbound Delivery Created 80000121

Step 12: Pick Outbound Delivery & Post Goods Issue

Navigate to Item Details

Enter Pick Quantity & Storage Location details and click on Apply

Click Save

Click Post GI

Check PO History

Check Accounting Entries for GI document.

BSX - Credit Entry

GBB - Debit Entry

Check In-Transit Stock (Transaction: MB5T)

Check Stock in MMBE

Step 13: Post Goods Receipt in Receiving Plant

Post Goods Receipt can be done against Purchase Order or against outbound delivery with 101 movement type.

Check PO History

Check FI Entries for GR document

BSX - Debit Entry

WRX - Credit Entry

Step 14: Check the document flow (Transaction: VL03N or Manage Outbound delievries App)

Step 15: Invoice Verification in Receiving Company Code

Check PO History

Check Follow on documents for invoice

KBS - Credit Entry

WRX - Debit Entry

Check the stock in MMBE

Step 16: Create Billing Document

Check Document flow

Click the below link for Intra-company purchasing process.

Intra-Company Purchasing in S/4 HANA: A Guide to Efficient STO Processes

 

 

Regards, 

Leelamohan Mannem

entry.title: Effortless Session Management for AI-Powered Applications Using Redis on SAP BTP
entry.content:
The below content was enhanced and reviewed with the help of AI tools.

This blog post is part of the:

"Unleashing the Power of Redis on SAP BTP for Modern Applications:A Blog Series"

Session management refers to the process of securely handling user sessions in a web or application environment. Session management is all about keeping user sessions safe and sound in a web or app. A session is like a temporary chat between a user and the app, usually lasting from the time they log in until they log out or take a break.

Why is it important? 

Session management is critical for:

  • Authentication: Keeping users logged in securely.
  • State persistence: Maintaining shopping carts, user preferences, and ongoing activity, for example.
  • Security: Ensuring sessions are private and limited in scope.

AI-powered applications such as chatbots, recommendation engines, and virtual assistants rely heavily on maintaining user context to deliver a personalized and coherent experience. Session management plays a pivotal role in this, especially in multi-turn interactions or applications with dynamic content. However, traditional session storage systems often struggle with the speed, scale, and flexibility required by modern AI use cases. 

Why session management matters in AI applications?

In AI workflows, sessions are more than just data containers—they serve as continuity bridges. Consider the following scenarios:

  • Conversational AI: LLM-based chat applications need to maintain chat history and user preferences across multiple exchanges.
  • Recommendation Systems: Personalization engines must track session-level signals like clicks or product views.
  • Real-time Co-pilots: Applications that assist users (e.g., code generation, writing suggestions) need to preserve state mid-task.

The challenge: AI workloads generate and consume state rapidly, requiring a session management layer that is both high-throughput and low-latency.

Why Redis on SAP BTP?

The Redis service excels as a session store because of the following reasons:

  • Sub-millisecond latency: essential for user-facing, real-time applications.
  • In-memory performance: store and access data directly in memory.
  • Flexible data structures: Hashes, Lists, JSON, and Streams are ideal for storing structured session data.
  • Built-in expiration (TTL): Automatically cleans up stale sessions.
  • Horizontal scalability: with Redis cluster option architecture.

Redis on SAP BTP is part of the BTP cloud services portfolio and can be used securely to support any SAP scenario as part of  your CPEA/BTPEA license agreement, you can also test the service without any cost using the free tier service plan option.

Some common patterns for AI session management:

  •  Chatbot Session Context: use Redis hashes/json to store message history, user profile, and intent state.
Data model example (JSON):
{
  "session_id": "sessionid1234",
  "user": {
    "name": "Paul",
    "language": "EN"
  },
  "history": [
    {"user": "Hi", "xyzbot": "Hello, how can I help you today?"},
    {"user": "Whow is life?", "xyzbot": "Life is like the weather—unpredictable. When it is dark and miserable, we must have hope."}
  ]
}
  • LLM Prompt Caching: Store intermediate prompt completions to reduce latency and cost
  • Key: prompt_hash 
  • Value: cached_response 
  • TTL: Based on prompt freshness
  • Session sharding: For high-traffic applications, shard sessions across nodes using Redis clusters.
  • Use consistent hashing for session keys

Implementation Walkthrough

Let’s walk through a sample chatbot session manager using Redis and Python (with redis-py client and redis.commands.json):

from redis import Redis
from redis.commands.json.path import Path
r = Redis(host='localhost', port=6390, decode_responses=True)
session_id = "session:sessionid1234"

# Create or update a session
session_data = {
  "user": {"name": "Paul", "language": "EN"},
  "history": []
}
r.json().set(session_id, Path.root_path(), session_data)

# Append to history
history = r.json().get(session_id, Path(".history"))
history.append({"user": "Hi", "xyzbot": "Hello, how can I help you today?"})
r.json().set(session_id, Path(".history"), history)

# Set TTL to expire in 45 minutes
r.expire(session_id, 2700)

Architecture: AI Chatbot with Redis Session Layer

  • User interacts with chatbot frontend
  • Backend invokes LLM APIs
  • Redis stores/retrieves session context

Information sources used in this blog.

  • Redis on SAP BTP
  • Redis Documentation
  • RedisJSON Module
  • redis-py GitHub
  • OpenAI GPT API Usage Guidelines

I hope you enjoyed this reading. If you liked it, you might also want to check out the other blogs in the same series.

Regards, Antonio

entry.title: Real-world use cases where the use of Redis on SAP BTP brings extra value
entry.content:
Some parts of the content below were enhanced and reviewed with the help of AI tools.

This blog post is part of the:

"Unleashing the Power of Redis on SAP BTP for Modern Applications:A Blog Series"
 
SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) is the foundation for building and integrating intelligent enterprise applications. While it offers a wide range of powerful services like SAP HANA, SAP Integration Suite, SAP Event Mesh, and AI Core, it also offers open-source based solutions like Redis on SAP BTP — a high-performance in-memory data store — that unlocks even more value for developers looking to build fast, scalable, and responsive applications.
In this blog post, we explore typical use cases where Redis on SAP BTP brings extra value, especially when integrated with extensions to SAP and to innovation scenarios.
 
Why Redis + SAP BTP?

SAP BTP provides a unified environment for developing, managing, and deploying applications and services across SAP and third-party systems. While SAP BTP delivers enterprise-grade capabilities, Redis complements it with:

  • Real-time data access: Sub-millisecond latency for mission-critical workloads.
  • High-performance caching: Reduces load on backend systems and speeds up response times.
  • Flexible data models: Support for strings, hashes, lists, sets and more.

Redis is available through the SAP BTP marketplace, making integration straightforward and secure. You can start with the free-tier service plan offering  without any costs.

Redis meets SAP BTP: real-world use cases that shine

In the rapidly evolving digital landscape, performance and scalability are not optional—they’re mission-critical. SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) offers a robust foundation for building enterprise-grade applications, but what happens when you inject Redis into the mix? You unlock real-time speed, seamless scalability, and architectural flexibility that elevate SAP BTP to a whole new level.

 
Below some examples of uses cases to demonstrated where Redis on SAP BTP can bring value:

1. Real-Time Inventory and Supply Chain Visibility

Scenario: A global retailer can use SAP S/4HANA and SAP BTP to manage inventory across thousands of locations.

Redis on SAP BTP in action: can serve as a real-time caching layer for inventory data. 

Impact:

  • Faster API responses for stock checks
  • Streamlined fulfilment workflows
  • Higher customer satisfaction through accurate availability

2. Personalized Digital Commerce Experiences

Scenario: An e-commerce app needs to deliver personalized experiences in real time.

Redis on SAP BTP in action: stores session data, user profiles, and recommendation metadata, proving high-performance access to other applications responsible for delivering AI-powered recommendations based on user behavior and preferences..

Impact:

  • Sub-millisecond personalization
  • Increased customer engagement
  • Higher conversion rates

3. Accelerated Finance & Risk Reporting

Scenario: A multinational finance institution aggregates data from various SAP systems for real-time financial dashboards.

Redis on SAP BTP in action: caches precomputed several metrics and aggregates, using for example sorted lists and sorted sets to handle time series data for time-based KPI data.

Impact:

  • Rapid dashboard load times
  • Reduced strain on SAP backend systems
  • Enhanced decision-making speed

4. IoT-Driven Asset Management

Scenario: A worldwide manufacturing company uses SAP Asset Intelligence Network to manage and monitor remote machinery.

Redis on SAP BTP in action: operates as an edge cache for sensor readings at region level, making available the data for central telemetry processes in real-time to support predictive maintenance capabilities.

Impact:

  • Reduced equipment downtime by accessing quickly to relevant data
  • Optimized operational efficiency
Integration patterns and best practices
  • Sidecar Cache with SAP CAP: Use Redis on SAP BTP alongside with SAP CAP services to offload repetitive queries.
  • SAP BTP Marketplace Integration: Provision Redis on SAP BTP from the SAP BTP marketplace for seamless setup.
  • Security & HA: Use Redis on SAP BTP built-in security capabilities, scaling and high availability features.
  • Architectural Simplicity: Avoid architectural sprawl by consuming Redis on SAP BTP directly from your SAP BTP accounts available in different hyperscaler and regions.

Conclusion

Redis on SAP BTP enhances SAP BTP by offering additional performance, setup simplicity, and scalability in key scenarios — from caching to session management support. It’s especially valuable for developers building modern, high-performance enterprise applications that demand real-time responsiveness and elasticity.

As enterprise workloads become increasingly cloud-native and data-driven, Redis on SAP BTP serves as a foundational component that complements SAP BTP cloud services portfolio.

I hope you enjoyed this reading. If you liked it, you might also want to check out the other blogs in the same series.

Regards, Antonio

entry.title: What If Redis Powered ChatGPT? Here’s What AI Thinks
entry.content:
Some parts of the content below were enhanced and reviewed with the help of AI tools.

This blog post is part of the:

"Unleashing the Power of Redis on SAP BTP for Modern Applications:A Blog Series"

Have you ever wondered what it would look like if Redis—known for its blazing-fast in-memory performance and data capabilities—played a role in powering ChatGPT architecture? We wanted to know too. So we went straight to the source, and ask ChatGPT itself.

AI talking about AI

In this post, we asked the AI: ChatGPT to imagine a world where Redis its part of the core of its software architecture. The result? A surprisingly insightful breakdown of how Redis could enhance performance, scalability, and real-time interaction in large language model (LLM) applications.

From session management and caching to pub/sub messaging and low-latency data access, here’s what AI has to say about teaming up with Redis:

- - - - Begin of ChatGPT responses - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - -

— 🤖 What Does a ChatGPT SaaS Platform Need?

A typical ChatGPT SaaS product serves multiple tenants (e.g., businesses or developers), each sending thousands of user prompts per day. It must:

  • Handle real-time conversations
  • Enforce rate limits and usage quotas
  • Cache responses efficiently
  • Provide scalable inference services
  • Manage sessions and context across interactions

A caching service like Redis fits into this puzzle perfectly!

— 🔧 What are the Redis Use Cases in ChatGPT SaaS ?

Here’s how Redis could support ChatGPT key architecture components:

1. ⚡️ Caching for Faster Responses

Redis can cache frequent prompts and their responses, static assets, or metadata from the model (e.g., instruction templates). This reduces calls to the inference engine and improves performance.

Benefit: Lower latency and compute costs.

2. 👥 Session Management

For active conversations, you need fast access to short-term context or message history. Redis can store:

  • Auth tokens, conversation context windows and TTL-based session data.

Benefit: Persistent and fast context storage across messages.

3. 🚦Rate Limiting & Quota Enforcement

Use Redis counters with INCR and EXPIRE to track usage per user or tenant, applying:

  • API rate limits, token quotas and tiered pricing logic.

Benefit: Real-time usage enforcement and abuse prevention.

4. 🧵 Task Queuing & Async Processing

Redis Streams or Lists can queue background jobs like:

  • Logging, usage billing, email alerts and external API callbacks.

Benefit: Scalable, decoupled task processing using tools like Celery or RQ.

5. 🔍 Vector Similarity Search (for RAG)

With Redis Stack, you can store embeddings and perform semantic search for:

  • Document retrieval, RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) and multi-turn context augmentation.

Benefit: Fast k-NN vector search with filtering and metadata support.

6. 📊 Real-Time Analytics and Monitoring

Redis can also be used to track:

  • Real-time user activity, system health metrics, billing and usage dashboards

Benefit: Immediate observability and business intelligence.

- - - - End of ChatGPT responses - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - -

Resume the features and value for SaaS

Looking at the above topics, we can summarise it in the following table and observe that ChatGPT SaaS successfully identified a value proposition for its architecture in terms of the most relevant features of Redis.

Redis Role

Feature Used

Value to SaaS

Caching

Key-value store

Faster responses, reduced compute load

Session Management

Hashes, TTL

Short-term memory across chats

Rate Limiting

INCR, EXPIRE

Quota enforcement, anti-abuse

Async Task Queue

Streams, Lists

Offloads heavy or slow tasks

Vector Search (RAG)

Redis Stack, VSS

Semantic document retrieval

Real-Time Analytics

Pub/Sub, Sorted Sets

Business dashboards and system metrics

If we used Redis on SAP BTP offering, based on the native hyperscaler offerings (Azure Cache for Redis, Amazon Elastic Cache for Redis, GCP Memorystore for Redis and AliCloud ApsaraDB for Redis) it is possible to cover mostly of the above mentioned roles. ☝️The exception comes only to roles that will need persistence (not available in SAP service offering ) and vector search (feature not included in all options of native Redis hyperscaler offerings).

In the end we asked ChatGPT to list all sources of information used in its answers, see below ChatGPT’s response:

"

  • Redis Official Documentation.
  • Redis Stack for Vector Search.
  • General SaaS and System Design Patterns ( Load-balanced micro-services | Redis in multi-tenant API systems | Usage from cloud platforms: AWS/GCP/Azure).
  • LLM & Chatbot Architectures: (OpenAI best practices | Inference and prompt orchestration designs).
  • No external sources or APIs were accessed during this chat; all content is based on trained knowledge as of June 2024.

"

entry.title: Re: Intercompany Financial Posting (1GP) - What is it use...
entry.content:
Dear user,

And thank you for asking your question on the SAP Community blog.

Here are the following answer I can provide regarding your questioning :

Can 1GP be used for goods or services?
No, 1GP is not typically used for goods or services that require VAT or formal invoicing.

Is it only appropriate when there’s no VAT involved?
Yes, 1GP is best for non-VAT transactions between company codes.

Realistic business scenarios for using 1GP:

  • Internal cost allocations (e.g., shared overheads)
  • Transferring funds or assets between company codes
  • Settling internal balances.

I hope my explanation is clear enough and answer your main questions.

Do not hesitate to come back to me if you need more details.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Integrating PostgreSQL and Redis on SAP BTP for high performance scenario
entry.content:
Some parts of the content below were enhanced and reviewed with the help of AI tools.

This blog post is part of the:

"Unleashing the Power of Redis on SAP BTP for Modern Applications:A Blog Series"

Imagine the fowling business scenario:

  • High-Performance E-Commerce app developed and supported using SAP BTP cloud services.

An e-commerce app requires a high-performance backend architecture that efficiently handles user sessions, product catalog queries, and order transactions.

How can we use both solutions to support our solution architecture:

  • PostgreSQL on SAP BTP:  Can be used as the primary relational database for storing structured data such as users, orders, and product details.
  • Redis on SAP BTP: Can be used for caching frequently accessed data (e.g., product details, user sessions) and implementing rate limiting to prevent excessive requests.

A typical e-commerce application will have the following architecture layers:

  1. Client Layer: Users interact with the platform via web app or mobile app
  2. Load Balancer: Routes requests to backend instances and ensures scalability.
  3. Application Layer: a set of backend services handle business logic, process API requests, and interact with databases.
  4. Caching Layer (Redis): Stores frequently accessed data (e.g., product listings, user sessions) to reduce database load.
  5. Database Layer (PostgreSQL): Stores structured data such as users, orders, and product catalogs.
  6. Integration with External Services like: Payment Gateway for handling transactions and Search Service (for efficient product search.

In a High-Performance E-Commerce application, leveraging Redis on SAP BTP service as a caching layer provides several tangible benefits that directly impact speed, scalability, and user experience. Here’s a breakdown of the key advantages of using the service in this scenario:

Ultra-Fast Performance

  • Why it matters: E-commerce users expect instant page loads, especially during high-traffic events like Black Friday. Redis is in-memory, meaning it serves data directly from RAM with sub-millisecond latency. This allows for real-time access to product listings, inventory status, and pricing info, pre-cached —crucial for fast browsing and decision-making.

Reduced Load on Primary Databases

  • Why it matters: Backend databases (like PostgreSQL, SAP HANA) are always expensive to scale and can become bottlenecks. Redis absorbs repetitive read operations (e.g., fetching top-selling products), drastically lowering the query volume hitting your database. This leads to cost savings and more stable performance under load.

Scalable Session Management

  • Why it matters: User sessions (shopping carts, preferences, login state) are core to personalization and checkout flows. Redis supports distributed session storage with persistence and replication. It’s ideal for multi-region deployments and horizontal scaling with minimal latency impact. TTL (Time-to-Live) functionality makes it easy to auto-expire inactive sessions, reducing memory overhead.

Flexible Data Structures

  • Why it matters: E-commerce logic can benefit from more than just key-value storage. Redis supports advanced types like Lists, Sets, Sorted Sets, and Hashes. For example you can use Sorted Sets for real-time leaderboards (e.g., “most viewed items”), and Hashes to store product metadata (like name, price, stock) efficiently.

Expiry and TTL Controls

  • Why it matters: E-commerce platforms deal with constantly changing data (flash sales, limited-time offers). Redis allows setting custom expiration per cache entry.vEnsures stale data doesn’t linger, maintaining accuracy without heavy invalidation logic.

Real-Time Analytics and Recommendations

  • Why it matters: Knowing what users interact with in real time helps drive sales.vUse Redis to track clickstream data, view counts, or search terms.This data can feed real-time recommendation engines or marketing automations.

Support for Pub/Sub and Streams

  • Why it matters: Real-time event processing enhances customer experience. Redis’ Pub/Sub and Streams allow broadcasting events like “product back in stock” or “price drop” notifications instantly. Supports event-driven architectures without introducing a separate messaging system.
  • I also suggest exploring SAP BTP services such as SAP Advanced Event Mesh, which offers a comprehensive event streaming solution with the added advantage of built-in integration with SAP systems.

High Availability and Durability

  • Why it matters: Downtime and data loss are costly in e-commerce. With Redis cluster option, apps can stay resilient even if individual nodes fail.

Easy Integration with Modern Stacks

  • Why it matters: Fast development and iteration cycles are key in a competitive market. Redis is supported by virtually every major language and framework (Node.js, Python, Java, Spring, etc.). Is compatible with common infrastructure setups (Docker, Kubernetes, AWS, Azure, GCP). Redis on SAP BTP is a multi-hyperscaler and multi-region offering available on AWS, Azure, GCP, ALI, NS2(USA).

I hope you enjoyed this reading. If you liked it, you might also want to check out the other blogs in the same series.

Regards, Antonio

entry.title: Dynamic Rate Limiting for Adaptive AI Business Scenarios Supported by Redis on SAP BTP
entry.content:
Some parts of the content below were enhanced and reviewed with the help of AI tools.

This blog post is part of the:

"Unleashing the Power of Redis on SAP BTP for Modern Applications:A Blog Series"

Using AI at business scale needs smarter control

AI capabilities are becoming more and more embedded in everyday business applications, from smart assistants to intelligent document workflows, enterprises are experiencing usage patterns that are both high-volume and highly variable. Fixed or one-size-fits-all rate limiting are not the best solution, businesses need dynamic, adaptive controls that can support real-time context and protect valuable AI resources without degrading the users experience.

What is dynamic rate limiting?

Dynamic rate limiting is known as a real-time method of controlling traffic or usage based on changing conditions. Unlike other methods that set fixed limits independent of the context, the dynamic rate limiting is supposed to be able to adjust based on conditions, for example:

  • User identification, type or subscription
  • System load or response latency
  • Tokens used, inference time (directly linked to AI service cost)
  • Business SLAs
  • Other relevant business factors

In AI systems computational and financial cost of running an AI model can vary widely, by having the possibility to do dynamic rate limiting becomes critical, also to protect the most critical business processes to not be disrupted by lacking of fast AI responses, with impact in company revenue and customers/users satisfaction/experience.

This is where a cache-service like Redis on SAP BTP can be highly effective, with its in-memory and high-speed data structures and real-time processing, provides a powerful foundation for dynamic rate limiting—one that aligns with business value, user behavior, and AI compute cost.

Redis on SAP BTP can give a complete control over rate limiting logic—is possible to implement algorithms like:

  • token bucket: can help tracking tokens using a key-value store (e.g., a counter) and timestamps
  • leaky bucket: using a queue/list with timestamps to simulate water leaking at a fixed rate
  • sliding window: with sorted sets to store request timestamps
  • or rolling logs: lists to keep a log of request timestamps per user or API key enabling the check of the list length or timestamps to decide if a request is allowed.

Redis on SAP BTP can be also integrated directly into app code making it easier to tie rate limiting to business logic, like charging tokens, quota handling, or user session scoring.

Typical API management solutions usually offer limited, one-size-fits-all rate limiting. Dynamically adjust limits based on user tiers, past behavior, or even AI model feedback loops—can be something hard to do with rigid API gateway rules. 

API gateways struggle to implement such responsive, context-aware mechanisms without significant complexity or external orchestration.

Redis on SAP BTP operates in-memory with sub-millisecond latency. This makes it ideal for high-performance, real-time applications (e.g., generative AI APIs, financial platforms, gaming, etc.), where even slight delays from external API management layers could hurt UX or the AI response time based on the conditions validation.

Architecture: cache-service as the dynamic control layer

Below is an example of simplified high-level architecture showing cache-service in a dynamic rate limiting setup:

The API gateway or middleware access Redis to decide whether a request should proceed, be throttled, or rerouted. Redis stores in-memory with high performance access the usage counters and dynamic thresholds based on time, plan, cost and other business related.

Below a sample Python code showing the implementation of rate-limiting mechanism using Redis, specifically employing a fixed window counter approach. This method tracks the number of requests a user makes within a defined time window and restricts access if the count exceeds a specified limit.

import redis
import time

r = redis.Redis(host='localhost', port=6379, db=0)

def can_access(user_id, token_cost, window_sec=60, max_tokens=100):
    key = f"rate:{user_id}:{int(time.time()) // window_sec}"
    current = r.get(key)
    usage = int(current) if current else 0

    if usage + token_cost > max_tokens:
        return False
    else:
        pipe = r.pipeline()
        pipe.incrby(key, token_cost)
        pipe.expire(key, window_sec)
        pipe.execute()
        return True

# Example usage:
user_id = "premium_user_123"
if can_access(user_id, token_cost=12):
    print("Access granted: calling AI model")
else:
    print("Rate limit exceeded")

I hope you enjoyed this reading. If you liked it, you might also want to check out the other blogs in the same series.

Regards, Antonio

entry.title: Leaderboard & Gamification in AI-Driven Apps using Redis on SAP BTP
entry.content:
Some parts of the content below were enhanced and reviewed with the help of AI tools.

This blog post is part of the:

"Unleashing the Power of Redis on SAP BTP for Modern Applications:A Blog Series"

Gamification has evolved beyond simple points and badges, when integrated with AI, gamified systems become dynamic, adaptive, and highly engaging—whether you are improving user retention in a learning app, driving customer loyalty in e-commerce, or just optimizing employee performance in enterprise tools.

In this blog post, we’ll explore how Redis on SAP BTP, running on SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP), can be used to build real-time leaderboards and gamification systems that are AI-aware and scalable. 

Why use Redis on SAP BTP for gamification & leaderboards?

The caching service is uniquely suited for high-performance gamification mechanics:

  • Sub-millisecond latency for real-time score updates
  • Sorted Sets for building dynamic leaderboards
  • Pub/Sub or Streams for notifying users of score changes
  • Reliable and scalable with high-availability and clustering
  • Seamless integration with AI services via Redis on SAP BTP

AI meets gamification

AI can enhance gamification by:

  • Personalizing challenges or rewards
  • Adapting difficulty levels dynamically
  • Detecting anomalies (e.g., cheating or bots)
  • Segmenting users by skill or engagement

Redis on SAP BTP being a caching-service will act as a fast-access memory store for AI models to read/write gamification data in real time.

Example of a simplified solution architecture

See below a high-level architecture diagram illustrating how the caching-service integrates with AI and app components:

  • Frontend: Shows real-time leaderboards, badges, and progress bars.
  • Backend: Handles game logic and writes to Redis.
  • AI: Continuously reads/writes player stats to model engagement or performance.

Sorted sets: powering the leaderboard

Redis’s Sorted Sets (ZSETs) are ideal for leaderboards. Each player ID is associated with a score and automatically sorted.

Example: add or update a player’s score using redis-py phyton client

import redis

r = redis.Redis(host='your-redis-host', port=6379, decode_responses=True)

# Add or update user scores
r.zincrby("game_leaderboard", 150, "player:alice")
r.zincrby("game_leaderboard", 200, "player:bob")

Get Top N players

top_players = r.zrevrange("game_leaderboard", 0, 4, withscores=True)
for rank, (player, score) in enumerate(top_players, 1):
    print(f"{rank}. {player} - {score}")

Player’s rank

rank = r.zrevrank("game_leaderboard", "player:alice")
print(f"Alice's Rank: {rank + 1}")

SAP BTP Integration Pattern

On SAP BTP, Redis service can be provisioned via the SAP BTP Cloud Foundry runtime, your app backend (running in SAP BTP) can then:

  • Access Redis instance via secure service binding
  • Use AI Core for inference models that personalize user experiences
  • Use event service for asynchronous communication when achievements unlock

Benefits of using Redis on SAP BTP

  • 🔄Real-time response for highly interactive apps
  • 🔐Secure, managed service with SAP BTP integration
  • 💡Easy to plug into AI/ML components for adaptive gamification
  • ☁️Scalable across multiple app instances and geographies

I hope you enjoyed this reading. If you liked it, you might also want to check out the other blogs in the same series.

Regards, Antonio

entry.title: Unleashing the Power of Redis on SAP BTP for Modern Applications: A Blog Series
entry.content:
In today’s digital world, building applications that work well on any device and can grow with your company business is no longer a cool thing—it is a must. Whether you are using real-time data to make smart decisions or handling huge amounts of data, the data you process and manage is super important.

That’s where Redis on SAP BTP comes in and can help

On this blog series about Redis on SAP BPT! We’ll be looking on how Redis can help developers and enterprise architects solve complex problems quickly, easily, and efficiently. In the next blogs, we will try to show you real-world use cases, examples and patterns that show how Redis on SAP BTP is especially good at handling demanding, data-heavy applications.

Why Redis on SAP BTP?

Redis is more than just a high-performance in-memory data store. Its versatility—ranging from caching, session management, real-time rankings, real-time processing and distributed coordination—makes it ideal to support modern workloads.

Redis on SAP BTP Meets AI

Artificial Intelligence presents a new set of requirements: low-latency access to large volumes of data, efficient feature stores and many others. we’ll explore how Redis on SAP BT can power AI use cases.

Redis on SAP BTP: The Best of Both Worlds

This view wouldn’t be complete without looking at Redis in the context of SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP). Redis on SAP BTP combines speed and flexibility with the security, scalability, and integration capabilities of SAP’s enterprise-grade platform. Whether you’re developing extensions for SAP applications or building standalone intelligent apps, Redis on SAP BTP gives you the foundation to move fast and scale confidently.

In Summary

Redis on SAP BTP is a powerful solution for supporting the new demand of AI architectures because of its low-latency, high-speed data access, real-time processing, and distributed computing capabilities, it can enhance AI performance across various domains.

Check below the blog posts:

  • Real-world use cases where the use of Redis on SAP BTP brings extra value

Enjoy the reading.

entry.title: Redis on SAP BTP - PostgreSQL Triggers Updating Redis with PL/pgSQL
entry.content:
This blog post explores how to combine PostgreSQL on SAP BTP and Redis on SAP BTP, both running as services on SAP BTP, to deliver fast, consistent updated data. Using PL/pgSQL, we'll build a trigger that notifies an external service to update Redis in real time—without needing language extensions like plpython or an external event broker or event management service.

Use case

(This is a simplified example intended only to illustrate the core idea)

Challenge: Slow inventory reads from the relational database during peak traffic affect user experience.

Solution: Use Redis as a fast cache for inventory levels, and automatically update it when PostgreSQL

inventory changes—triggered via a simple HTTP call from PostgreSQL to a microservice.

Components

Component Role

PostgreSQL on SAP BTPSource of inventory data
Redis on SAP BTPIn-memory cache for high-speed lookups
Update Service

Microservice that checks update events from PostgreSQL and writes to Redis

App Frontend Reads inventory from Redis

Step-by-Step implementation

1. Inventory table definition

CREATE TABLE inventory (
    product_id UUID PRIMARY KEY,
    quantity INTEGER NOT NULL,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT now()
);

Description: This is the main table that holds product inventory data in PostgreSQL. Each product has a unique identifier (product_id), a stock quantity, and a timestamp to track when the record was last updated.

2. Queue table for Redis sync

CREATE TABLE redis_update_queue (
    id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    product_id UUID NOT NULL,
    quantity INTEGER NOT NULL,
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT now()
);

Description: This table acts as a lightweight message queue within PostgreSQL. Whenever an inventory record is updated, a new row is added to this queue to record the product_id and its new quantity. The queue decouples database operations from Redis writes, ensuring that inventory changes are recorded and processed asynchronously.

Why the use of 'redis_update_queue'?

  • Avoids direct external calls (like to Redis) from inside the database
  • Ensures no data loss if Redis is temporarily unavailable
  • Enables retry logic and better observability
  • Keeps PostgreSQL pure PL/pgSQL-compatible—ideal for SAP BTP’s managed DB services
  • Makes it easier to scale with batching or message broker integration in future

3. Trigger function (PL/pgSQL)

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION queue_redis_update()
RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
BEGIN
    INSERT INTO redis_update_queue (product_id, quantity)
    VALUES (NEW.product_id, NEW.quantity);
    RETURN NEW;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;

Description: This PL/pgSQL trigger function is  to be executed every time a row in the inventory table is updated. It inserts the new inventory values (product_id and quantity) into the 'redis_update_queue' table. This step ensures that all changes are recorded without any external dependencies or delay in the transactional path.

4. Create trigger

CREATE TRIGGER trigger_redis_update
AFTER UPDATE ON inventory
FOR EACH ROW
EXECUTE FUNCTION queue_redis_update();

Description: This trigger binds the function queue_redis_update() to the inventory table. It’s set to fire after each row update, ensuring every change to inventory results in a corresponding queue entry. This makes the update-to-cache process automatic and invisible to the application logic.

5. Redis update microservice (Python example)

import time
import psycopg2
import redis

pg_conn = psycopg2.connect("dbname=postgres user=admin password=xxx host=pg-host")
r = redis.Redis(host='redis-host', port=6379, password='yourpass', decode_responses=True)

while True:
    with pg_conn.cursor() as cur:
        cur.execute("SELECT id, product_id, quantity FROM redis_update_queue ORDER BY id LIMIT 1")
        row = cur.fetchone()
        if row:
            r.hset("inventory", str(row[1]), row[2])
            cur.execute("DELETE FROM redis_update_queue WHERE id = %s", (row[0],))
            pg_conn.commit()
        else:
            time.sleep(1)

Description: This Python script acts as a worker process that continuously polls the 'redis_update_queue' table. When it finds a new entry:

  • It updates the inventory hash in Redis (r.hset(...))
  • Deletes the processed entry from the queue to avoid reprocessing
  • Sleeps for a short interval if no new records are found

This service ensures eventual consistency between PostgreSQL and Redis with minimal load on either system.

🛠You can deploy this script as a containerized microservice on SAP BTP (e.g., Kyma or Cloud Foundry), and scale it horizontally if needed. Learn on how to deploy microservices using SAP BTP here.

Potential advantages of using the solution

BenefitValue
PostgreSQL remains ACID-compliantNo external dependencies required
Redis delivers high-speed readsGreat for traffic-heavy read ops
Microservice keeps things decoupledEasy to retry, monitor, scale
Pure PL/pgSQL—no external language dependenciesCompatible with SAP BTP PostgreSQL
 
Thanks for reading! Be sure to check out what our product team has shared about Redis and PostgreSQL:
  • PostgreSQL on SAP BTP - Are you looking for official blog posts? Blog INDEX
  • Redis on SAP BTP - INDEX of official blog posts
Regards, 
Antonio

entry.title: 聴講者/登壇者視点からの【SAP Inside Track Tokyo 2025 Event Report】
entry.content:
3日間わたって開催され開催された、SAP Inside Track Tokyo 2025 (sitTokyo2025)に3日間オンサイト参加しました。こちらの記事では、聴講者の視点で報告させていただきます。
一言でいえば、楽しく知識をアップデートできる場でした。これまで参加されたことのない方、次回ぜひ参加してみてはいかがでしょう。

なお、
　イベント概要はSAP Inside Track Tokyo 2025 Event Information　(Sumikko さん)
　イベント全体総括はSAP Inside Track Tokyo 2025 Event Report　(ume_haccaさん)　を参照ください。

また、JSUG テクニカル部会　部会長 石原（ベニックソリューション）および筆者はDay1  Session4 「JSUGテクニカル部会およびASUG Tech Connectの紹介」に登壇ましたこちらに関しては以下記事をご参照ください。

sitTokyo2025 Day1:JSUGテクニカル部会およびASUG Tech Connectの紹介【Part1】
　　前半 「JSUGテクニカル部会の紹介＋SAP サポート 中国 （大連） 交流・訪問」

sitTokyo2025 Day1:JSUGテクニカル部会およびASUG Tech Connectの紹介【Part2】 
　　後半 「ASUG Tech Connectの紹介(フィードバック)」

 



会場について

このイベントリモートとオンサイトのハイブリッドで開催されましたが、私は３日間ともオンサイトで参加しました。オンサイトの会場はSAPジャパン本社のミーティングルームです。SAPジャパンの本社は2022年にこちらに移転してきたとのことですが、ひろびろ・きれいで快適に過ごすことができます。
　参考：新オフィスの面積を55%削減、SAPジャパンが目指す理想の働き方とは?

少し古めのオフィスに勤務する筆者からはうらやましい限りです。

セッション開始まで

セッションが行われるミーティングルームに入ると、運営の皆さんが機器のチェックなどあわただしく動かれていました。会場の真ん中にアルコールと軽食が用意していただいており、運営の皆さんが「お酒を飲みながらリラックスして参加してくださいねー」など、私を含めて参加者に暖かく声をかけていただき、コミュニティを広げる気遣いをとても感じながら、”ぼっち”感（2日目と3日目は”ぼっち”で参加）も少しづつ薄らぎます。

いよいよセッション開始

オンサイト聴講者が座る横には、全体統括やモデレーター、配信などの機器コントロールされる方、音楽や合いの手？をタイムリーに流す方、会場のロジ担当されている方など、様々な役割の運営の皆さんが座っていらっしゃいます。開始時間が近づくとともに、ぼっちで参加している聴講者の筆者より、運営の皆さんの緊張感が高まるのがわかります。裏方と表（セッション）を同時に見れるというのはオンサイト参加の一つの楽しみ方？かもしれません。

セッション開始

セッションはまずはアイスブレークからスタートします。リアルタイムアンケートをつかって参加者皆さんからの声を集めつつ、モデレーターの方が上手にアンケート結果を拾って講演者に投げかけ会話を弾ませます。参加者も参加しているぞという感覚を持てるともに、登壇者の緊張もほぐれていくように見えます。

セッション

セッションはテーマ・進め方・難易度どれをとってもバラエティ豊かなものでした、”そうそう”と共感できる内容、言葉しか聞いたことが無かった技術の説明、ただ共通していることの一つは登壇者皆さんが、何らかを伝えようという工夫と熱意をもってプレゼンされていることでした。なにより知的好奇心をそそられるコンテンツばかりでした。

セッション終了

セッションが終了すると、参加者皆さんからのフィードバックタイム。暖かいコメントが多く（セッション内容が素晴らしいので当たり前ですが）ほっこりします。登壇者さんのリラックスした表情はセッション開始前とは大きく違う気がします。

全てのセッション終了～Closing/Warp

毎回４～６のセッションが終わるとそろそろクロージング、リアルタイムアンケートツールのイベント全体に対する満足度スコアを少しはらはらしなが注目している運営者の皆さんの様子が、ちょっとほほえましかったです。どの日もほぼ満点の点数が出ていました。
ここまでで、リモート参加者の方とはお別れとなり、残りはオンサイト参加者のお楽しみ。大？抽選会が開かれます。運営の皆さんが諸々工夫をして集めてきて下さったノベルティや書籍などをじゃんけん大会でゲットしていきます。賞品の数も多いので、かなりのオンサイト参加者が賞品をうけとれたのではと思います。筆者も書籍（e-book)を頂きました。

抽選会終了後はネットワーキングタイム、セッション数が少なかった最終日（3日目）はを除くと、限られた時間ではありましたが、登壇者への質問や、聴講者同士の交流など短い時間を惜しむように皆さん交流されていました。

全体を通して

運営の皆さんの熱意と相違工夫により、コミュニティで学ぶ楽しさを実感することができる、そして、また行ってみたいと心から思えるイベントでした。運営だけでなく、セッション内容一つ一つの個性が強いところも、飽きずに楽しめる理由の一つかと思います。また、是非参加させていただければと思います。
運営の皆様、登壇者の皆様、聴講者のお疲れ様でした。また、ありがとうございました。

文責：JSUGテクニカル部会オピニオンリーダー　松井　憲司
　　　　　（株式会社NTTデータウェーブ）

最後までお読みいただきありがとうございました。
PS　JSUGテクニカル部会にもぜひお越しください。

entry.title: sitTokyo2025 Day1:JSUGテクニカル部会およびASUG Tech Connectの紹介【Part2】
entry.content:
この記事では、先日開催されたイベントSAP Inside Track Tokyo 2025　Day1  Session4 「JSUGテクニカル部会およびASUG Tech Connectの紹介」の後半 「ASUG Tech Connectの紹介(フィードバック)」について概要を紹介します。

2025年2月28日(金)、3月14日(金)、3月19日(水)3日間わたって開催され開催された、SAP Inside Track Tokyo 2025 (sitTokyo2025)にJSUG テクニカル部会　部会長 石原（ベニックソリューション）さま　と登壇しました。
　イベント概要はSAP Inside Track Tokyo 2025 Event Information　(Sumikko さん)
　イベント全体総括はSAP Inside Track Tokyo 2025 Event Report　(ume_haccaさん)　を参照ください。

なお前半、JSUGテクニカル部会の紹介JSUG テクニカル部会　部会長 石原（ベニックソリューション）　さまによるご講演はこちらをご覧ください。

 

JSUGテクニカル部会の海外派遣について

JSUGテクニカル部会では、新技術の探索の他現地ユーザとの交流を目的として毎年海外派遣を行っています。昨年度は一昨年に引き続き、世界最大のSAP User Community ASUG(北米のSAPユーザ会）主催する、ASUG Tech Connect（2024年11月West Palm Beach,USA）に４名を派遣しました。

 

ASUG Tech Connect 2024について

ASUG CEO & Chief Community Champion　Geoff Scott氏によれば、10月にバーチャルで開催されたSAP TechEd のフォローアップ的な位置付けであり、SAP TechEd で発表された内容を実際に体験できる場であり、SAP のテクノロジー専門家にとって、ハンズオンでテクノロジーを理解するための最良の場であるとしています。特に、SAP Sapphire との違いについて、参加者同士がつながり、互いの話を聞くことができる点を強調しています。

セッションは　6つのセッションタイプ　×　7つの専門トラック　で構成され、計100以上のセッションが開かれました。
セッションタイプのうち「Innovation Spotlight」は他のSAPユーザーが最新のSAPテクノロジーを使用して得た実際の経験やベストプラクティスの共有の場となっており、TechEDとは少し異なるASUGならではの講演を聴講することができます。

 

ASUGによるのAI意識調査

1日目のKeynoteはTechEDを振り返るといった内容でしたが、2日目のKeynoteではASUG Researchによるユーザ企業の意識調査が発表されました。

87% の回答者がAI は産業革命を起こすと考え、48% が AI の利点を認識しているとうAIに対してポジティブな意見が多いことが紹介された一方、実際に活用しているユーザは32%にとどまるなど、期待は高いが本格活用の広範な普及は、米国でもこれからといった状況でした。学習データの重要性とSAPに蓄積されたデータの可能性について言及は興味深いものでした。

 

Joule’s ABAP Developer Capabilities

※sitTokyoでは時間の都合上一部割愛したパートです

筆者は、派遣されたJSUGメンバの中で、Application Development & Automationエリアを担当し、数あるキーワードの中でも、Joule’s ABAP Developer Capabilities　と　BTP and Fusion Development　にフォーカスしました。

Joule’s ABAP Developer Capabilitiesでは、「SAP S/4HANAとクリーンコアについてすべてを知っているAIベースの副操縦士」 というビジョンに基づき開発されたSAPのAI Co-pilot（Joule）によるABAP開発能力の向上について紹介されていました。

【Joule’s ABAP Developer Capabilities　主要な機能】

TRANSFORM
Classic ABAP code into ABAP Cloud–レガシーABAPをCloud準拠のABAPに書き換える支援

EMPOWER
Developers to infuse AI into business scenarios
–AI機能をカスタムABAPアプリケーションに組み込むためのソフトウェア開発キットがリリース

ACCELERATE
Increase development efficiency leveraging generative AI assistance in the ABAP development tools
–Creation of ABAP Cloud apps including test cases and data（アプリケーションと単体テストケースの作成）
–Code explanation（コードの説明）
–Code prediction（コードの補完）
–Generation of unit tests for ABAP classes and　core data services (CDS) views
–Generation of business objects and services of　ABAP RESTful application programming model

ASUG CEO & Chief Community Champion　Geoff Scott氏の言葉を借りると

ABAP開発者がより高品質なコードをより短時間で作成するのに役立つ可能性がある。 これにより、開発効率が向上し、ビジネス価値の実現を加速させることができると期待している

ABAPコードのドキュメント作成を支援する可能性も秘めている
他の開発者が書いたコードを理解するのは難しい場合があるが、AIツールを使えば、コードの意図をより明確に理解することができると考えている。

ECCからS/4への移行を加速化するためにも活用できると期待している。
多くの企業がS/4への移行を進めている中で、AIツールが移行プロセスを効率化し、リスクを軽減するのに役立つと考えている。

といったものです。一方

ABAP開発者は、より効率的に開発を進めることができると同時に、生成されたコードの品質に責任を持つ必要がある。AIツールはあくまでも支援ツールであり、最終的な責任は開発者にあることを強調していました。

そして、SAPがABAP AIについて今後どのような機能を提供するのか、注目している。AIによるABAP開発は発展途上の分野であり、SAPが今後どのような機能を提供するのか、期待を寄せている。としています。

 

BTP and Fusion Development

※sitTokyoでは時間の都合上割愛したパートです

SAP BTP上へのローコード、ノーコード開発機能のリリース(SAP Build、SAP Build APPS)により、ビジネスユーザー（市民開発者）が自らがエンタープライズアプリケーションを作成しプロセスを自動化するハードルが下がるとともに、ABAP Cloud（SAPが提供するクラウド環境におけるABAP開発モデル）により、クラウド環境で本格的なABAP開発を行うことができるようになることで、全世界で200万人いるといわれるABAP開発者の能力をCloudに解き放ちさらに、ABAP開発環境がSAP Buildと統合されることによりプロコード開発とのよりシームレスな開発環境が整い、市民開発者とプロフェッショナル開発者との統合開発（FusionDevelopment）による企業内のイノベーションの加速を促していく可能性について紹介されていました。 

 市民開発者とプロフェッショナル開発者との統合開発だけでなく、異なる技術（UI開発者によるSAP Build でのMobile アプリ開発　と　Java and JavaScrip開発者がSAP Build CodeでバックエンドロジックやAPI連携などを実装する）などFusionDevelopmentのモデルケースも紹介されていました。

不足しがちなプロフェッショナル開発者とその知見(特に業務知見)を補うコンセプトと感じました。

BTP活用にチャレンジする組織

少し直接的な技術から離れた、ASUGらしいセッションの一つとして、BTP活用にチャレンジする組織としてのコーニング社によるSAP BTP C4E (Center for Enablement)　立ち上げ事例が紹介されていました。

コーニング社がSAP BTPプラットフォーム導入を成功させるため、同社のTom Gee氏が中心となり、社内の関係者を連携させる取り組みが紹介されています。 彼はまず、BTPセンターオブエネーブルメント（C4E）を設立し、情報共有と協調を促進しました。 この活動は徐々に拡大し、多様な部門からの参加を得て、BTPに関する知識と利用を組織全体に広めています。 チームの構成や運営方法、今後の計画なども説明されており、社内でのSAP BTPの普及と活用を推進する様子が示されました。

 

2025年のGlobalEventについて

2025年は、以下のGlobalEventが発表されています。皆さんも参加されてはいかがでしょうか。

SAP Sapphire & ASUG Annual Conference May 19–21 Orlando, USA
SAP TechEd Nov 4–6 Berlin, DEU
ASUG Tech Connect Nov 4–6 Kentucky ,　USA

 

もう少し詳しく知りたい方へ

紙面の都合上、概要のみのご紹介となりますが、sitTokyo2025での講演の模様（動画）や、より詳細な発表を行ったJSUGテクニカル部会での講演資料を以下で公開していますので、ご興味のある方は確認してみてください。

sitTokyo2025での講演の模様（動画）はこちら 　※YouTubeサイトが開き音声が出ます。

JSUGテクニカル部会での講演資料はこちら 
　※資料ダウンロードにはJSUGNetへのログインが必要です。
　　事前にJSUGNET利用登録が必要となります。
　　JSUGNET利用登録は、JSUG会員企業の従業員は無料で登録可能です。
　　くわしくはJSUGのFAQをご覧ください。

entry.title: sitTokyo2025 Day1:JSUGテクニカル部会およびASUG Tech Connectの紹介【Part1】
entry.content:
この記事では、先日開催されたイベントSAP Inside Track Tokyo 2025　Day1  Session4 「JSUGテクニカル部会およびASUG Tech Connectの紹介」の前半 「JSUGテクニカル部会の紹介」について概要を紹介します。

2025年2月28日(金)、3月14日(金)、3月19日(水)3日間わたって開催された、SAP Inside Track Tokyo 2025 (sitTokyo2025)にJSUG テクニカル部会　部会長 石原（ベニックソリューション）さま　と登壇しました。
　イベント概要はSAP Inside Track Tokyo 2025 Event Information　(Sumikko さん)
　イベント全体総括はSAP Inside Track Tokyo 2025 Event Report　(ume_haccaさん)　を参照ください。

前半は、JSUGテクニカル部会の紹介　
　　JSUG テクニカル部会　部会長 石原（ベニックソリューション）　さまによるご講演です。

なお、後半「ASUG Tech Connect 2024 Feedback」についてはこちらを参照してください。

 

JSUGテクニカル部会について　～部会概要～

JSUGテクニカル部会は、JSUG内に複数ある部会のうちの、テクニカル領域を担当する部会として、以下を目的とした活動を行っています。

  • SAPシステムに携わる実務者層を中心とした相互交流の場とする。（Network）
  • 実務者層の中でも役割別に分科会を立ち上げ共通課題を共に勉強・研究する。（Education）
  • SAPシステムを技術視点で研究・勉強し、SAPへフィードバックする機会を設ける。（Influence）

 

SAP技術者といっても、30年以上の経験者から最近初めてSAPに触ったという方など、皆さんバックグラウンドが異なることを考慮して、以下のバランスを考慮した活動に留意しています。

  • 主にエキスパートを対象とした新技術探究 (For Expert)
  • 主に初心者を対象としたSAPの歩き方 (For Beginner)

また、テクニカル領域は、他の領域（産業領域、機能領域）を支える領域として関係が深いことから、

  • 他の部会やコミュティとの交流 (For User Groups)

等、最近は他の部会やコミュティとの連携も重視しています。

昨年度は、ワーキンググループによる技術習得や情報交換活動の他、TechEDなどでの国際派遣と新技術探訪、そしてSAP サポート 中国 （大連） 交流・訪問等の活動を行いました。

 

SAP サポート 中国 （大連） 交流・訪問等の活動

SAP技術を担当された方であれば、SAPサポートにインシデントを登録された経験がある方は多いと思います。システムを介しさらに母国語が違う中でのコミュニケーションによって、障害の重大さなどがうまく伝わらない、など歯がゆい経験をされた方も多いかと思います。一方、サポート側も同じ生身の人ですので同様な思いされているようです。そこでテクニカル部会は、ユーザとSAPサポートとの相互理解を深ることで、インシデントの解決スピードやサポート品質および新サービスのさらなる向上を図る目的で毎年「SAPサポート中国大連エンジニア交流企画」を行っています。 

昨年度は、しばらくぶりにSAPサポート中国（大連）を訪問しオンサイトでの交流を再開しました。日本からは味の素、アンリツ、イーグル工業、ダイキン、ベニックソリューション（川崎重工子会社、部会長）、SAPジャパンの７社８名、現地SAP Support Center Chinaではサポートセンター長含む50名に迎えていただきました。

２日間の日程では、AI活用がサポートの分野でも進んでいることなどの最新情報紹介や、

グローバルなバックオフィスチームによる迅速なサポートで積極的に問題解決を図り、SAP の知識とエクスペリエンスへのアクセスを 24 時間 365 日無制限に提供するSAP Mission Control Centerの紹介、インシデントの解決スピードを上げるためのポイントのケースタディ等実務面での有意義な情報交換・ディスカッションの他、懇親会や中国伝統を学ぶとった文化を通じて、相互理解をより深める有意義な訪問なりました。

 

もう少し詳しく知りたい方へ

紙面の都合上、概要のみのご紹介となりますが、sitTokyo2025での講演の模様（動画）や、より詳細な発表を行ったJSUGテクニカル部会での講演資料を以下で公開していますので、ご興味のある方は確認してみてください。

sitTokyo2025での講演の模様（動画）はこちら 　※YouTubeサイトが開き音声が出ます。

JSUGテクニカル部会での講演資料はこちら 
　※資料ダウンロードにはJSUGNetへのログインが必要です。
　　事前にJSUGNET利用登録が必要となります。
　　JSUGNET利用登録は、JSUG会員企業の従業員は無料で登録可能です。
　　くわしくはJSUGのFAQをご覧ください。

entry.title: Re: Generate IDOC from SAP BTP CAP app
entry.content:
Hi,

Assuming Cloud connector is in place , at ECC use function module MASTER_IDOC_DISTRIBUTE

FUNCTION Z_TRIGGER_IDOC.
*"----------------------------------------------------------------------
*"  Importing  VALUE(I_MATNR) TYPE  MATNR
*"----------------------------------------------------------------------

DATA: ls_edidc TYPE edidc,
      lt_edidd TYPE TABLE OF edidd,
      lv_idoc_number TYPE edi_docnum.

CALL FUNCTION 'MASTER_IDOC_DISTRIBUTE'
  EXPORTING
    master_idoc_control = ls_edidc
  TABLES
    communication_idoc_control = lt_edidd
  EXCEPTIONS
    OTHERS = 1.

ENDFUNCTION.

and in the CAP application:-

import { executeFunction, rfcDestination } from '@sap-cloud-sdk/rfc';

const destination = await rfcDestination({ destinationName: 'ECC_RFC' });

const response = await executeFunction(destination, {
  functionName: 'Z_TRIGGER_IDOC',
  parameters: {
    I_MATNR: '123456'
  }
});

Thanks,

Arun
